---
title: "small decisions big risk"
format: html
---

## Small Decisions, Big Risk

Many high-risk moments are built by small choices that seem harmless at first: skipping meals, isolating, passing a triggering location, avoiding support calls, or telling yourself "I can handle it."

Tracking these choices helps you interrupt risk earlier and choose safer alternatives.

Recovery is strengthened by planning ahead, not relying on willpower alone in high-pressure moments. Many setbacks follow predictable patterns: rising stress, narrowing options, and automatic decisions that increase risk.

Structured planning interrupts that chain. By identifying risk earlier and choosing concrete responses, you reduce decision fatigue and increase the chance of staying aligned with your goals.

::: callout
### Instructions
List recent small decisions that moved you closer to risk.
:::

| Small decision | How it increased risk | Better decision next time |
|---|---|---|
| | | |
| | | |
| | | |
| | | |
